original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Venous thromboembolic diseases: diagnosis, management and thrombophilia testing
original_registry_entry.version: NICE guideline NG158, last updated 2 August 2023
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: People presenting with signs or symptoms of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism and people receiving related follow-up.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial medical assessment and clinician-led diagnostic pathways for suspected venous thromboembolic disease.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting a swollen or painful leg history, physical examination, conditional DVT or PE risk features, and clinician-entered assessment context. It does not calculate Wells or PERC scores, infer DVT or PE, order tests, prescribe anticoagulation, or determine urgency.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.2 and Table 1 — DVT history, examination, and clinician-scored features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting swollen or painful leg context, general medical history, physical examination, laterality and swelling or tenderness findings, immobility, surgery, malignancy, previous DVT, and a clinician-entered score only. No score is calculated.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.15–1.1.17 and Table 2 — PE history, examination, and clinician-scored features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting chest pain, shortness of breath, haemoptysis, vital-sign and examination context, prior VTE, immobilisation or surgery, malignancy, and a clinician-entered score only. No diagnosis or score is inferred.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.2.4 — clinician-agreed monitoring and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an already agreed monitoring or follow-up plan and information already provided for confirmed or suspected low-risk PE; it does not generate that plan.

title: Venous thromboembolic diseases: diagnosis, management and thrombophilia testing
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: People presenting with signs or symptoms of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism and people receiving related follow-up.
intended_setting: Initial medical assessment and clinician-led diagnostic pathways for suspected venous thromboembolic disease.
